content.problemStatement:
You are given a weighted simple undirected graph with NNN vertices, which are numbered from 111 to NNN. You are guaranteed that vertex 111 is not an articulation point (which means that even after removing the vertex 111, the remaining graph is connected) and has an edge connecting it to each of the other N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 vertices.

For each K‚àà{1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1}K \in \{1, 2, \ldots, N-1\}K‚àà{1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1} you need to find the minimum weight of a spanning tree where the degree of vertex 111 is equal to KKK.

###Input:


The first line of input contains two integers N,MN, MN,M: the number of vertices and edges in the graph.
Each of the next MMM lines contains three integers Ui,Vi,WiU_i, V_i, W_iUi‚Äã,Vi‚Äã,Wi‚Äã, denoting an edge between vertices UiU_iUi‚Äã and ViV_iVi‚Äã with weight WiW_iWi‚Äã.
###Output:
Print N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 integers in a single line -  the minimum weight of a spanning tree where the degree of vertex 111 is equal to 1,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí11, 2, \ldots, N-11,2,‚Ä¶,N‚àí1.

###Constraints


2‚â§N‚â§100‚Äâ0002 \leq N \leq 100\,0002‚â§N‚â§100000
2N‚àí3‚â§M‚â§200‚Äâ0002N - 3 \leq M \leq 200\,0002N‚àí3‚â§M‚â§200000
1‚â§Wi‚â§200‚Äâ0001 \leq W_i \leq 200\,0001‚â§Wi‚Äã‚â§200000
1‚â§Ui<Vi‚â§N1 \leq U_i < V_i \leq N1‚â§Ui‚Äã<Vi‚Äã‚â§N
It is guaranteed that the graph contains no multiple edges and vertex 111 is not an articulation point and its degree is equal to N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1.
